element_introduction_order.element: basic locomotion (movement in all directions, jumping)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: immediately required for platforming sections with water hazards
obtained by hitting boulders scattered throughout stages
instant-death platforming sections requiring precise jumping and flight
improved range and spread over stone axes
obtained from defeated enemies via boulders; lost on hit
enables crossing water hazards safely; enables mobility in stages with vertical scrolling demands

element_introduction_order.element: hidden item tile / Macho Riki transformation
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: transforms player into powerful form; fires projectile clones of self (Riki Lariat attack)
concludes each stage; final stage contains four sub-stages with bosses

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: aggressive vertically-scrolling challenge with brief respite upon power-up collection; tension sustained through environmental hazards (instant-death water) and enemy projectiles; relief cycles tied to boss defeat and stage completion

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: boulders scattered throughout stages conceal power-ups and hidden item tiles—destruction required to access
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: hidden item tile triggers transformation into Macho Riki form with enhanced attack; specific tile location not documented

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1 introduces platforming with water hazards and basic enemies; establishes weapon upgrade system and power-up dependency
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 2-3 escalate enemy density, projectile patterns, and introduce higher-tier weapon upgrades (torches); hidden Macho Riki transformation increases complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 4 consists of four sub-stages with bosses; cumulative difficulty spike as checkpoint system resets on death (player loses all power-ups and returns to specific checkpoint)
notable_spikes: Stage 1: instant-death water platforming with moving/submerging lilypads
Stage 4: final stage boss gauntlet (four sub-stages)
Any hit: weapon and speed downgrades force adaptation mid-combat

notable_level_patterns.description: Stage 1 establishes pattern of water bodies requiring precise jumping or flight capability; lilypads move unpredictably, creating timing-based navigation challenge that serves as gating mechanic for flight power-up utility

Boulders scattered throughout stages must be destroyed to reveal health and power-ups; forces active engagement with environment rather than passive traversal; creates micro-decisions about weapon use timing

Death returns player to stage checkpoint but strips all power-ups and weapon upgrades; creates high-stakes environment where progression damage (hit downgrades) accumulates toward stage restart; incentivizes avoiding hits

sources_used: wikipedia: Adventures of Dino Riki (Gameplay section; Development section)
tcrf: Adventures of Dino Riki (transformation/regional differences documentation)
